message:
fix: CLI retry sends error context, Bun.which for cross-platform PATH

1. CLI retry was useless — sent identical prompt on every attempt. The
   non-CLI path includes "[PREVIOUS ATTEMPT FAILED]" and bumps temp on
   retry, but the CLI path just re-ran the same call. Now includes the
   truncated previous error so the model can self-correct (e.g., fix a
   schema violation on the second try).

2. Replaced Bun.spawnSync(["which", tool]) with Bun.which(tool) for
   cross-platform PATH lookup. The `which` command doesn't exist on
   Windows (only `where`). Bun.which() works on all platforms, is
   synchronous, and avoids subprocess overhead.

3. Explicit cliCommand values are now validated via Bun.which() before
   being accepted. Previously "cliCommand: bad-tool" would pass
   resolution and only fail at Bun.spawn time with a confusing OS error.

4. Removed unverified codex --quiet flag — only claude -p is verified
   to support stdin piping. Unknown tools use empty flags (safe default).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
